Professional Experience

Work & Research

Engineering projects and research contributions in water resources and GIS.

🏢
Senior Environmental Engineer
Streams Tech Ltd (USA) — Remote
January 2023 – Present
🌊
Flood Risk Assessment · Virginia, USA
Four Mile Run (FMR) Watershed Management Program 2023
Delineated the watershed for the study area in Virginia using ArcMap and GeoSWMM. Merged existing drainage network with the delineated network to run the hydrodynamic model for different return periods. Performed risk assessment at flood-affected points.
ArcMapGeoSWMMHydrodynamic ModelingFlood Risk
🏙️
Urban Drainage · Bangladesh
Gazipur City Corporation (GCC) Drainage Modelling
Developed a geospatial Storm Water Management Model (SWMM) and simulated rainfall-runoff for effective catchment and sub-catchment areas. Designed alternative drainage measures for flood mitigation in Gazipur City.
GeoSWMMRainfall-RunoffDrainage DesignGIS
🌆
Resilient Cities · Urban Water
Strengthening Capacity for Livable and Resilient Cities
Developed SWMM model for drainage and flood analysis. Assessed capacity of existing drains and developed flood extension maps. Created decision support scenarios for each study zone.
SWMMFlood MappingDecision Support
🔬
Graduate Research Assistant
Dept. of Environment, Water Resources and Coastal Engineering
January 2021 – December 2022
🛰️
GIS & Remote Sensing Research
Old Buriganga River — GIS Analysis & Remote Sensing
Monitored spatial movement and morphological changes of the Old Buriganga River Bankline using Google Earth images 2001–2019. Calculated NDVI and NDWI spectral indices for assessing land use changes and vegetation-water variations.
GISNDVINDWIGoogle Earth
💧
GIS & Remote Sensing · DSAS
The Digital Shoreline Analysis System (DSAS) of Jamuna River
This project analyzes long-term morphological changes of the Jamuna River by examining bankline shifting, erosion, and deposition patterns between 1990 and 2020 using geospatial analysis. Based on the observed historical trends, predictive spatial modeling was applied to estimate potential bankline shifts over the next 10 and 20 years.
DSAS
🗺️
ArcSWAT · Remote Sensing
SWAT Analysis of Matamuhuri River 
This project applies the ArcSWAT model to simulate rainfall–runoff processes in the Matamuhuri River watershed. The model was calibrated using observed streamflow data (2008–2016) to evaluate its ability to reproduce peak flows and annual discharge patterns.
SWAT, CN, LULC
